job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process. The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

This is a strategic and technical role. It is not an SEO support role, a blogging role with keyword research added on, or a task-execution position within a larger team.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval (outreach team identifies candidates, you approve/reject based on quality standards)
  • SEO performance reporting (organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, Search Console health)

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role - Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content - Experience managing technical SEO independently - Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture - Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool - Experience working across US and/or UK markets - Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including AI-powered search surfaces - Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset - Thinks in systems, not tasks (goal: a compounding organic engine) - Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments - Analytically honest, able to diagnose and adjust independently - Collaborative but not passive (works closely with content and development teams while driving the agenda)

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content
  • Candidates who treat Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain high-intent vs. informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on tools
  • Candidates with no exposure to US/UK search behaviour who treat all markets as equivalent

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic SEO role focused on building a scalable organic growth system for US/UK markets, requiring technical expertise in programmatic SEO and commercial intent-driven content architecture.

In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking.   • Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing for web and mobile applications across browsers, devices, and environments.

  • Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps, evidence, and severity classification.
  • Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to maintain stability during releases.
  • Maintain test documentation, execution reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking.

  • Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers to clarify requirements and resolve issues.
  • Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews).

  • Own assigned modules or features to ensure testing completeness and release readiness.

Required Skills & Mindset:

  • Willingness to deeply understand business logic before testing.
  • Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond standard use cases.
  • Analytical mindset and curiosity to question unexpected system behavior.
  • Clear written/verbal communication and collaboration skills.

  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment.

  • Curiosity about test data management (synthetic/test data generation).
  • Experience testing mobile applications or cross-platform systems.

Contact: Share your resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Detail-oriented QA Engineer with 1–3 years of experience in manual testing, test case creation, and defect tracking. Collaborates with teams to validate web/mobile apps, supports Agile/Scrum, and gains exposure to automation and AI testing.

job_description:
Business Analyst

The Business Analyst plays a key role in ensuring requirements are clearly understood, accurately documented, and traceable throughout the software development lifecycle. This role focuses on translating business and functional inputs into structured, standardized documentation that supports effective development, testing, and delivery. Working closely with business analysts, design teams, and technical stakeholders, the role ensures consistency, clarity, and alignment across all requirement artifacts.

Key Responsibilities

  • Create Software Requirement Specifications (SRS) based on inputs from Core Business Analysts, stakeholder discussions, and requirement walkthroughs
  • Prepare Business Requirement Documents (BRD) using approved templates while ensuring business intent and system behavior are clearly documented
  • Participate in requirement discussions to understand business objectives, functional flows, dependencies, and assumptions before documentation
  • Analyze requirements to identify gaps, ambiguities, and inconsistencies, and work with Core BAs to resolve them
  • Document functional flows, business rules, and acceptance criteria where applicable to support development and QA teams
  • Document wireframes, UI flows, validations, and UI specifications in collaboration with design and product teams
  • Maintain change logs, version history, and document updates throughout the project lifecycle
  • Create and manage traceability matrices linking business, functional, and technical requirements
  • Maintain cross-references across BRD, SRS, UI, API, and related documentation
  • Ensure documentation consistency, completeness, and alignment with approved scope and delivery timelines
  • Ensure accurate and timely PMS updates with structured documentation details
  • Support development, QA, UAT, and audit processes through clear, traceable, and up-to-date documentation
  • Assist in requirement reviews, walkthroughs, and validation sessions with internal and client stakeholders

Required Skills and Experience

  • 1 to 3 years of experience in Business Analysis or related roles
  • Strong experience in BRD and SRS creation
  • Hands-on experience with documentation templates, standards, and version control
  • Ability to document UI wireframes and functional flows clearly
  • Working knowledge of APIs and system integrations for documentation purposes
  • Experience with project management systems and documentation tools
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple documents in parallel
  • Excellent written communication and stakeholder coordination skills

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
  • 1 to 3 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Position Summary

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Process Expert to lead the mapping, definition, and optimization of our end-to-end medical billing workflows. The ideal candidate will bridge the gap between clinical data and financial outcomes, ensuring our processes—from patient intake and coding to claims submission and denial management—are documented, scalable, and compliant.

Key Responsibilities

  • Process Mapping & Documentation: Create detailed "As-Is" and "To-Be" process maps for all RCM functions (Eligibility, Coding, Charge Entry, Claim Submission, AR Follow-up, and Payment Posting).
  • Workflow Optimization: Identify bottlenecks, manual redundancies, and friction points in the billing lifecycle to improve Clean Claim Rates (CCR) and reduce Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).
  • SOP Development: Draft and maintain a comprehensive library of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to ensure consistency across billing teams and facilitate faster onboarding.
  • Gap Analysis: Conduct regular audits of current workflows against industry benchmarks and regulatory requirements (HIPAA, CMS, and Payer-specific guidelines).
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with IT to automate manual tasks and with the Quality Assurance team to integrate compliance checks into the workflow.
  • Performance Metrics: Define Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for each process step and create dashboards to track process health and employee productivity.

Required Qualifications

  • Experience: 5+ years in Process Engineering or Operations Management, specifically within Healthcare RCM or Medical Billing.
  • Technical Skills: Proficiency in process mapping software (Lucidchart, Visio, or Miro) and deep familiarity with PM/EHR systems.
  • Methodology: Certification in Six Sigma (Green or Black Belt), Lean, or Kaizen is highly preferred.
  • Knowledge: Strong understanding of the claim lifecycle, including CPT/ICD-10 coding, EDI 837/835 transactions, and denial management strategies.
  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Administration, Business, or Industrial Engineering.

Core Competencies

  • Analytical Rigor: Ability to turn complex, messy data into clean, visual flowcharts.
  • Communication: Able to explain technical process changes to both frontline billers and executive leadership.
  • Adaptability: Comfortable working in the fast-changing landscape of payer rules and healthcare legislation.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at recruiter@aimagroup.co.uk.
